channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tenants Evicted After Ongoing Issues at Rutland Property
item.description: Two tenants have been evicted from a property on McCurdy Road in Rutland following months of disturbances and concerns raised by neighbours. Kelowna city councillor Mohini Singh, who owns a quarter of...

item.title: MSC to Develop 30-Hectare Container Terminal at Snake Island Port in Lagos
item.description: Na Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC), wey be one of di biggest shipping lines for di world, don tanda 45-year sub-concession agreement with Nigerdock to run, develop, and maintain one exclusive con...

item.title: Majority of Filipino Adults Support Law to Limit Political Dynasties
item.description: According to a Pulse Asia survey conducted from February 27 to March 2, 2026, 64% of Filipino adults are in favor of passing the anti-political dynasty law, which aims to limit the influence of politi...

item.title: Kenyan Musician Overcomes Visa Challenges and Thrives in the USA
item.description: Gearrie Wainaina, a talented Kenyan saxophonist and MC, has shared his long and challenging journey to the USA. Back in 2010, he first attempted to get a US visa after being nominated for an award alo...

item.title: Porto Coach Praises Terem Moffi for Impressive Performance Against Stuttgart
item.description: FC Porto manager Francesco Farioli don give big props to Nigerian forward Terem Moffi after him solid show for the UEFA Europa League game against VfB Stuttgart wey Porto win 2–1. Moffi wey join Porto...

item.title: London Witness Used Smart Glasses to Receive Coaching During Court Testimony
item.description: During a London High Court trial concerning the directorship of a property development company, a witness named Laimonas Jakstys was discovered using smart glasses connected to his mobile phone to rec...

item.title: QuTwo develops AI platform to help enterprises transition toward quantum computing
item.description: Finnish entrepreneur Peter Sarlin, who previously sold his startup to AMD for $665 million, has launched two new ventures: NestAI, a physical AI lab, and QuTwo, an AI company preparing enterprises for...

item.title: EACC Chair Calls on Students and Citizens to Fight Corruption in Kenya
item.description: On 13 March 2026, the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) Chairperson, David Oginde, addressed students and leaders at Pan Africa Christian University during the Kenya Leadership Integrity Fo...

item.title: Nehal Hashmi appointed as Sindh Governor amid political concerns
item.description: In a sudden political development, PML-N leader Nehal Hashmi is set to take oath as the new governor of Sindh today in Karachi, with Sindh High Court Chief Justice Zafar Ahmed Rajput administering the...

item.title: NDLEA Arrests Former Councillor, Seizes Large Quantities of Drugs Across Nigeria
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don carry out major operations across Nigeria, wey result in di arrest of several people involved in drug trafficking. One of di high-pro...

item.title: Key Developments Across Africa on 13th March 2026
item.description: On 13th March 2026, several important events unfolded across the African continent. In Eritrea, satirical cartoonist Biniam Solomon, also known as Cobra, was released after 15 years in detention witho...

item.title: Dubai Financial District Damaged by Intercepted Iranian Drone Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Dubai experienced significant disruption early on Friday after debris from intercepted Iranian drones caused damage to buildings in the International Financial Centre. Authorities confirmed that the d...

item.title: President Samia Emphasizes Protecting Ngorongoro Conservation Area for Future Generations
item.description: Tanzania President Dr Samia Suluhu Hassan emphasised the importance of conserving the Ngorongoro Conservation Area, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, during a meeting at Chamwino State House in Dodoma on ...

item.title: Fuel subsidy removal pushes more Nigerians into poverty, study shows
item.description: The removal of petrol subsidy by the Nigerian government, as announced by President Bola Tinubu on 29 May, 2023, don sharply push up prices for petrol and other goods, waka increase poverty levels acr...

item.title: FCCPC investigates airlines for overcharging passengers during festive season
item.description: The Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) don yan say some airlines wey dem catch dey increase ticket price anyhow during 2025 Christmas and New Year period fit face sanctions...

item.title: Satirical cartoon highlights absurdities in Australian politics
item.description: The article features a satirical cartoon by First Dog on the Moon, poking fun at Australian politics, particularly the National Party and figures like Matt Canavan. The cartoon exaggerates political b...

item.title: Women Employees Constitute Nearly Half of Workforce at VOC Port’s Berth 9 Terminal
item.description: At V.O. Chidambaranar Port’s Berth 9 in Thoothukudi, women have made remarkable strides in a traditionally male-dominated industry. Operating under a Public-Private Partnership model, Berth 9 has ensu...

item.title: R55,000 Raised to Support Boy Bullied at School Over Pie
item.description: A disturbing incident at Thembalethu Primary School in the Eastern Cape, where a young boy was embarrassed for not being able to afford a school pie, sparked nationwide outrage and compassion. The vid...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Support Public Disclosure of China’s Activities in West Philippine Sea
item.description: A recent Pulse Asia survey shows that a significant majority of Filipinos back the continued public disclosure of China's actions against Philippine vessels and fishermen in the West Philippine Sea (W...

item.title: Los Angeles man sentenced for shooting that struck LAPD helicopter tail
item.description: A South Los Angeles man, Douglass Byers, 62, was convicted of firing a rifle shot that pierced the tail of a Los Angeles Police Department helicopter in August 2025, an act prosecutors said nearly cau...
